title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but improper use or reliance on it without validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives. Additionally, if the tool is compromised or contains malicious code, it could introduce risks to users' environments.
recommended_actions: Conduct a thorough code review of the tool before integrating it into your workflows.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside other established vulnerability scanning solutions.
Monitor the tool's repository and community feedback for updates, bug reports, or potential security concerns.

title: Imager::File::GIF versions through 1.002 for Perl allow a heap out of bounds (OOB) write on...
description:
Imager::File::GIF versions through 1.002 for Perl allow a heap out of bounds (OOB) write on crafted multi-frame GIF files.

Imager::File::GIF's i_readgif_multi_low allocates a single per-row buffer GifRow sized for the GIF's global screen width 'SWidth' and reuses it across every image in the file.

The page-match branch validates Image.Width + Image.Left > SWidth before each DGifGetLine write, but the parallel skip-image branch at imgif.c:790-805 calls DGifGetLine(GifFile, GifRow, Width) with n

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Imager::File::GIF versions through 1.002 for Perl allows a heap out-of-bounds write when processing crafted multi-frame GIF files. This could lead to application crashes, data corruption, or potential remote code execution if exploited.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to a patched version of Imager::File::GIF (if available) or apply any security patches provided by the maintainers.
Implement input validation and sanitization to reject or safely handle untrusted GIF files.
Monitor systems for suspicious activity or crashes related to GIF file processing and apply runtime protections where possible.

title:
Apache::Session::Generate::SHA256 versions before 1.3.19 for Perl create insecure session ids.
...
description:
Apache::Session::Generate::SHA256 versions before 1.3.19 for Perl create insecure session ids.

Apache::Session::Generate::SHA256 generated session ids insecurely. The default session id generator returns a SHA-256 hash of the built-in rand() function, the epoch time, and the PID, that is hashed again. These are predictable, low-entropy sources. Predicable session ids could allow an attacker to gain access to systems.

Note that version 1.3.19 has a fallback without warning to use insecure sessi

title: The Notify Odoo plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in all versions...
description: The Notify Odoo plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Cross-Site Request Forgery in all versions up to, and including, 1.0.1. This is due to missing or incorrect nonce validation on the _updateSettings function. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to change the Notify Odoo URL to an attacker-controlled URL and modify notification, tracking image, and allowed IP address settings via a forged request granted they can trick a site administrator into performing an action such as cli

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to modify critical settings in the Notify Odoo plugin, potentially redirecting notifications and sensitive data to an attacker-controlled URL. This could lead to data leaks, phishing attacks, or unauthorized access to restricted resources.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using WordPress
recommended_actions: Update the Notify Odoo plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests targeting this vulnerability.
Educate administrators to avoid clicking on suspicious links or performing sensitive actions without verifying the source.

title: The Quick Playground plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Path Traversal in versions up to and...
description: The Quick Playground plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Path Traversal in versions up to and including 1.3.3. This is due to insufficient path validation in the qckply_zip_theme() function, which appends a user-controlled 'stylesheet' parameter directly to the theme root directory path without sanitizing directory traversal sequences. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to trigger the creation of a ZIP archive containing arbitrary files from the server's filesystem — includin

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6403.

title: A supply chain attack compromised the official installation packages of DAEMON Tools Lite ...
description: A supply chain attack compromised the official installation packages of DAEMON Tools Lite (Windows versions 12.5.0.2421 through 12.5.0.2434), distributed from the legitimate website daemon-tools.cc between approximately April 8, 2026, and May 5, 2026. Attackers gained unauthorized access to the vendor's (AVB Disc Soft) build or distribution infrastructure and trojanized three binaries: DTHelper.exe, DiscSoftBusServiceLite.exe, and DTShellHlp.exe. These files were digitally signed with the legiti

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The supply chain attack on DAEMON Tools Lite compromises the integrity of installation packages, potentially allowing attackers to execute malicious code on affected systems. This could lead to data breaches, ransomware deployment, or unauthorized access to sensitive systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately revoke the compromised digital certificates and issue advisories to users to avoid using the affected versions (12.5.0.2421 through 12.5.0.2434).
Encourage users to uninstall the compromised versions and provide a clean, verified version of the software for download.

Conduct a thorough investigation of the vendor's build and distribution infrastructure to identify and remediate the breach, and implement stronger security measures such as code-signing certificate monitoring and multi-factor authentication for developers.

title: The Classified Listing – AI-Powered Classified ads & Business Directory Plugin plugin for...
description: The Classified Listing – AI-Powered Classified ads & Business Directory Plugin plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access in all versions up to, and including, 5.3.10. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with subscriber-level access and above, to add arbitrary notes to any order and trigger unsolicited notification and moderation emails to listing owners without adminis

title: The Form Notify plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass in versions up to and...
description: The Form Notify plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Authentication Bypass in versions up to and including 1.1.10. This is due to the plugin trusting user-controlled cookie data to determine which WordPress account to authenticate after a LINE OAuth login. When LINE doesn't provide an email address (which is common), the plugin falls back to reading the 'form_notify_line_email' cookie value without verifying that the LINE account is associated with that email address. This makes it possible for

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5229.

title: The Frontend Admin by DynamiApps plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in...
description: The Frontend Admin by DynamiApps plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in versions up to and including 3.28.36. This is due to insufficient authorization checks in the role field update mechanism combined with overly permissive capabilities for the admin_form post type. The admin_form custom post type uses 'capability_type' => 'page', which grants editors the ability to create and edit forms. When an editor creates an edit_user form, they can manipulate the form configuratio

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6228.

title: CVE-2026-41498 - Kimai is an open-source time tracking application. Prior to version 2.54.0, the Team API endpoints u
description: Kimai is an open-source time tracking application. Prior to version 2.54.0, the Team API endpoints use #[IsGranted('edit_team')] instead of #[IsGranted('edit', 'team')], causing Symfony TeamVoter to abstain from voting. This removes entity-level ownership checks on team operations, allowing any user with the edit_team permission to modify any team, not just teams they are authorized to manage. This issue has been patched in version 2.54.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized users with the 'edit_team' permission to modify any team, bypassing entity-level ownership checks. This could lead to unauthorized changes to team configurations, potentially affecting operational integrity and data consistency.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Kimai to version 2.54.0 or later to apply the patch addressing this vulnerability.
Review and audit user permissions to ensure that only authorized personnel have the 'edit_team' permission.
Monitor team modification logs for any unauthorized changes and take corrective action if necessary.

title: CVE-2026-8128 - A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0. The affected element is an unkn
description: A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /admin/viewmsg.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument msgid results in sql injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8128.

title: CVE-2026-8127 - A vulnerability has been found in eladmin up to 2.7. Impacted is the function checkLevel of the file
description: A vulnerability has been found in eladmin up to 2.7. Impacted is the function checkLevel of the file /rest/UserController.java of the component Users API Endpoint. Such manipulation leads to improper access controls. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in eladmin's Users API Endpoint allows improper access controls, potentially enabling unauthorized access to sensitive user data or administrative functions. Since the exploit is publicly disclosed and can be executed remotely, the risk of exploitation is significant.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply access control policies at the API gateway level to restrict unauthorized access.
Monitor logs for unusual activity targeting the /rest/UserController.java endpoint and implement intrusion detection mechanisms.
Contact the project maintainers for updates or consider migrating to a more actively maintained framework if no patch is forthcoming.
